title: narcissus minor subsp. asturiensis
intro: Narcissus minor subsp. asturiensis, also known as the Asturian lesser narcissus, is a rare subspecies of the Narcissus minor, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Narcissus minor subsp. asturiensis include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and its antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically administered to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as to soothe sore throats and coughs.
